mnemonics: MA = Mean Adjustment (It smooths out data to reveal the underlying trend by averaging out short-term fluctuations)
Order 'Q' is the Queue of past errors (An MA(q) model uses a queue of the last 'q' forecast errors to predict the next value)
keyPoints: A moving average process, MA(q), is a linear combination of past white noise error terms.
MA processes are always stationary, as they depend only on a finite number of past shocks.
The autocorrelation function (ACF) of an MA(q) process cuts off after lag q, which helps in identifying the order of the model.
commonMistakes: Confusing Moving Average (MA) models with the simple moving average smoothing technique used for forecasting.
Overlooking the requirement for the error terms to be white noise (i.i.d. with mean zero and constant variance).
Attempting to model a non-stationary series with an MA process, which is designed for stationary data.
